subject: Geography
title: Vertical Cities
description: A micro-lesson that makes Vertical Cities usable.

hook.text: Hong Kong has over 9,000 skyscrapers—more than any other city on Earth.

buildup.text: When land is scarce, the only option is to build up. Hong Kong sits on rugged mountains.

discovery.text: Singapore reclaims land from the sea and builds underground to fit 6 million people.

twist.text: Dense vertical cities are more energy-efficient per person than sprawling suburbs.

climax.text: The world's densest cities prove that millions can thrive on tiny footprints.

punchline.text: When you can't spread out, you stack up.

quiz.question: Why does Hong Kong have so many skyscrapers?
options: Limited flat land forces building upward
It was a government design choice
